title: Cisco VLAN Fundamentals
description: Learn how to create VLANs, assign descriptive names, configure access ports, and verify VLAN assignments on a Cisco switch.

tasks.explanation: Privileged EXEC mode (Switch#) is required before viewing or changing VLAN configuration.

tasks.hint: Use the command: 'configure terminal' or 'conf t'
tasks.explanation: Global configuration mode is where VLANs are created and named on the switch.

tasks.prompt: Create VLAN 10 (or enter VLAN configuration mode for VLAN 10).
tasks.explanation: The 'vlan [id]' command creates a new VLAN and enters VLAN configuration mode. Valid VLAN IDs are 1-4094.

tasks.prompt: Assign the name 'Sales' to the current VLAN.
tasks.explanation: Naming VLANs helps administrators identify their purpose in show commands and network documentation.

tasks.prompt: Return to global configuration and create VLAN 20.
tasks.hint: Use 'exit' to leave VLAN config mode, then enter 'vlan 20'
tasks.explanation: You can enter VLAN configuration mode directly with 'vlan 20' from global config without explicitly exiting first.
Each VLAN can have a unique descriptive name up to 32 characters.

tasks.prompt: Enter Interface Configuration mode for FastEthernet0/5.
tasks.hint: Use the command: 'interface FastEthernet0/5' or 'int fa0/5'
tasks.explanation: Access ports are configured at the interface level. The prompt changes to Switch(config-if)#.

tasks.prompt: Explicitly set the interface as an access port.
tasks.explanation: Access ports carry traffic for a single VLAN. Explicitly setting mode access prevents the port from becoming a trunk.

tasks.prompt: Assign FastEthernet0/5 to VLAN 10 as an access port.
tasks.explanation: The 'switchport access vlan' command places the port into the specified VLAN for untagged traffic.

tasks.prompt: Enter Interface Configuration mode for FastEthernet0/6.
tasks.hint: Use the command: 'interface FastEthernet0/6' or 'int fa0/6'
tasks.explanation: You can navigate directly to another interface from interface configuration mode.

tasks.prompt: Assign FastEthernet0/6 to VLAN 20 as an access port.
tasks.explanation: Each access port belongs to exactly one VLAN. Devices on Fa0/5 and Fa0/6 will be in separate broadcast domains.

tasks.prompt: Display a summary of all VLANs and their port assignments.
tasks.explanation: 'show vlan brief' lists every VLAN, its name, status, and assigned ports—essential for verifying access port configuration.
